page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4n server family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 60.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that the 16 vCPUs deliver consistent compute performance without resource contention from other tenants. With no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included, network configurations must be managed through alternative routing or address allocation methods. The hardware profile is balanced for workloads requiring a moderate memory footprint per core alongside dedicated processing power.
This server type is suitable for general-purpose compute workloads, web serving, application servers, and moderate database tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-16 is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 60.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. Part of the c4n server family, this instance features dedicated CPU allocation to ensure consistent compute performance for demanding applications. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. Additionally, the instance comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource configuration makes the c4n-standard-16 suitable for general-purpose workloads, application hosting, and web serving where dedicated processing power is required without the need for specialized hardware acceleration or local solid-state drives.
